title: Add tests for TranscriptEntrySchema and TranscriptUuidSchema
description:
Task 9.3.10 added two new Zod schemas to transcript-schemas.ts but no corresponding tests were added:

1. TranscriptEntrySchema - minimal schema using z.object({}).passthrough() for raw transcript JSONL entries
2. TranscriptUuidSchema - schema for extracting UUID from transcript entries with z.object({ uuid: z.string().optional() }).passthrough()

These schemas are used in transcript-service.ts:
- TranscriptEntrySchema: getTranscript() line 378, processTranscriptFile() line 1037
- TranscriptUuidSchema: parseUuid() line 654

The existing test file packages/sidekick-core/src/state/__tests__/transcript-schemas.test.ts tests PersistedTranscriptStateSchema and CompactionHistorySchema but not these new schemas.

Files:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/state/transcript-schemas.ts (schema definitions)
- packages/sidekick-core/src/state/__tests__/transcript-schemas.test.ts (test file to update)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tests verify TranscriptEntrySchema accepts valid objects and rejects non-objects (null, arrays, primitives)
2. Tests verify TranscriptEntrySchema.passthrough() allows unknown fields
3. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema extracts uuid field correctly
4. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema returns undefined for missing uuid
5. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema rejects non-string uuid values
6. All tests pass

title: Phase 9: Refactoring & Architecture Improvements
description: Comprehensive refactoring to improve code quality, test coverage, and architectural consistency. See docs/ROADMAP.md for details.

title: 9.3 State Management Infrastructure
description: Centralize state access behind clean abstractions. Almost complete - just 9.3.10 remaining.
acceptance_criteria: All state writes use atomic pattern. Schema validation on all state reads. No direct path construction outside StateService. No direct fs read/write for state files outside StateService (UI exempted).
close_reason: All tasks completed: atomic writes, schema validation on all reads, centralized path construction

title: 9.3.10 Add Zod validation to all state reads
description: staging-service.ts (lines 204-205, 283-284), cli-staging-reader.ts (line 60-61), transcript-service.ts (lines 376, 643, 1023)
acceptance_criteria: Zod validation added to: staging-service.ts (lines 204-205, 283-284), cli-staging-reader.ts (line 60-61), transcript-service.ts (lines 376, 643, 1023). Tests verify schema validation catches malformed data.
close_reason: Added Zod validation to all state reads

title: 9.4 Config Source-of-Truth
description: Objectives: (1) YAML files are single source of truth for defaults, (2) Prevent configuration drift, (3) Clean code: no @deprecation, no backward compatibility needed. Lower priority - no issues found in 9.2 audit.
acceptance_criteria: No Zod .default() for configuration values. Config parse failures are hard errors.

title: 9.4.1 Audit Zod .default() calls and establish YAML as source
description: Find all Zod schemas with .default() calls. Ensure all defaults exist in YAML files in assets/sidekick/defaults/. Config loading fails hard if required values missing.
acceptance_criteria: All Zod .default() calls in config schemas identified and removed. Corresponding defaults exist in assets/sidekick/defaults/ YAML files. Config loading throws on missing required values.

title: 9.4.2 Add enforcement test: all config keys have YAML defaults
description: Test that prevents configuration drift by verifying all config keys have corresponding YAML defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Test exists that verifies all config keys have YAML defaults. Test fails if new config key added without YAML default.

title: 9.5 Feature Domain Consolidation
description: Objectives: (1) Move remaining feature code from daemon.ts to feature packages, (2) Clean code: no @deprecation, no backward compatibility needed. Note: 9.2.1 audit found handler architecture already Grade A - minimal work needed.
acceptance_criteria: daemon.ts has no reminder-specific logic or hardcoded reminder names. Feature packages own their event definitions. All 5 FIXME comments in structured-logging.ts resolved.

title: 9.5.1 Move P&R/VC state logic from daemon.ts to feature-reminders
description: Move pr-baseline.json writes (daemon.ts:624), vc-unverified.json/IPC handlers (daemon.ts:642-705), remove hardcoded reminder names from daemon core.
acceptance_criteria: P&R baseline management moved from daemon.ts:624 to feature-reminders handler. VC state management (vc-unverified.json, IPC handlers) moved from daemon.ts:642-705 to feature-reminders. No hardcoded reminder names in daemon.ts.

title: 9.5.2 Move event definitions to feature packages
description: Move from structured-logging.ts: ReminderConsumed, ReminderStaged, RemindersCleared → feature-reminders; SummaryUpdated, SummarySkipped → feature-session-summary. Resolves 5 FIXMEs.
acceptance_criteria: 5 event definitions moved: ReminderConsumed, ReminderStaged, RemindersCleared to feature-reminders; SummaryUpdated, SummarySkipped to feature-session-summary. 5 FIXMEs in structured-logging.ts resolved.

title: 9.6 Reminder Orchestration
description: Objectives: (1) Centralize 4 cross-reminder rules currently scattered across handlers, (2) Replace scattered deleteReminder() calls with declarative rule engine, (3) Clean code: no @deprecation, no backward compatibility needed.
acceptance_criteria: 4 cross-reminder rules in single declarative location. Handlers have single responsibility (no cross-reminder logic). Adding new reminder type doesn't require modifying existing handlers.

title: 9.6.1 Design ReminderOrchestrator rule engine
description: Declarative rules for 4 cross-reminder relationships: P&R staged→unstage VC, UserPromptSubmit→unstage VC or re-stage if unverified, VC consumed→reset P&R baseline, VC consumed→unstage P&R.
acceptance_criteria: ReminderOrchestrator class exists in feature-reminders with declarative rules for 4 cross-reminder relationships. Rules are data-driven, not scattered imperative code.

title: 9.6.2 Centralize P&R baseline state in orchestrator
description: Move pr-baseline.json management from IPC to orchestrator service. Clear read/write semantics for baseline state.
acceptance_criteria: pr-baseline.json management centralized in orchestrator. Clear API for reading/writing baseline state. No direct pr-baseline.json access outside orchestrator.

title: 9.6.3 Simplify handlers - remove scattered deleteReminder calls
description: Remove cross-reminder logic from stage-pause-and-reflect.ts, unstage-verify-completion.ts, inject-stop.ts. Handlers call orchestrator instead of direct coordination.
acceptance_criteria: deleteReminder() calls removed from stage-pause-and-reflect.ts, unstage-verify-completion.ts, inject-stop.ts. These handlers call orchestrator for cross-reminder coordination.

title: 9.7 Code Cleanup & Documentation Polish
description: Objectives: (1) Remove deprecated APIs and resolve remaining FIXMEs, (2) Documentation matches implementation, (3) Clean code: no @deprecation, no backward compatibility needed. Run after refactoring stabilizes.
acceptance_criteria: No @deprecated APIs remain. All FIXMEs addressed or converted to tracked issues. Design docs current with implementation.

title: 9.7.1 Remove deprecated APIs
description: initialize()→prepare()+start() (3 locations), getTranscriptService()→prepareTranscriptService() (2 locations), getSessionState()→getTranscriptMetrics() (1 location), SessionMetricsState type alias (2 locations).
acceptance_criteria: All deprecated APIs removed: initialize()→prepare()+start() (3 locations), getTranscriptService()→prepareTranscriptService() (2 locations), getSessionState()→getTranscriptMetrics() (1 location), SessionMetricsState alias (2 locations).

title: 9.7.2 Address remaining FIXMEs
description: structured-logging.ts:383 (extract event routing logic), transcript-service.ts:1439 (remove currentContextTokens backward compat), types/config.ts:15 (review minimal interface pattern).
acceptance_criteria: 3 FIXMEs addressed: structured-logging.ts:383 event routing extracted, transcript-service.ts:1439 currentContextTokens compat removed, types/config.ts:15 minimal interface reviewed.

title: 9.7.3 Documentation cleanup
description: Remove phase references from code comments. Update design docs if implementation diverged.
acceptance_criteria: No phase references in code comments. Design docs in docs/design/ match current implementation. No stale documentation references.

title: CLIStagingReader duplicates validation pattern without architectural comment
description:
The CLIStagingReader in feature-reminders duplicates the Zod validation pattern from StagingServiceCore but without documentation explaining why.

Context:
- StagingServiceCore.listReminders() logs validation failures via this.options.logger
- CLIStagingReader.listReminders() silently skips invalid files (no logger available in CLI context)
- Both use StagedReminderSchema.safeParse() correctly

The duplication exists because:
1. CLI process cannot access DaemonContext.staging (runs in separate process)
2. CLI has no logger instance available for warning messages
3. CLIStagingReader is a lightweight read-only interface for CLI consumption

This architectural decision should be documented in the file header or inline comments so future maintainers understand why the pattern differs.

Files:
- packages/feature-reminders/src/cli-staging-reader.ts

Related:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-service.ts (the daemon-side equivalent)
acceptance_criteria:
1. CLIStagingReader file header or class docstring explains why it exists separately from StagingServiceCore
2. Comment in listReminders() explains why validation failures are silent (no logger in CLI context)
3. No functional changes - documentation only

title: staging-service listReminders/listConsumedReminders bypass StateService
description:
In staging-service.ts, the listReminders() and listConsumedReminders() methods use direct readFile() calls instead of stateService.read(), violating the Phase 9.3 acceptance criteria 'No direct fs read/write for state files outside StateService'.

Context:
- stageReminder() correctly uses stateService.write() (line 130)
- readReminder() correctly uses stateService.read() (line 164)
- But listReminders() uses readFile() directly (line 204)
- And listConsumedReminders() uses readFile() directly (line 288)

This was discovered during code review of task 9.3.10 which added Zod validation. The validation was added correctly using StagedReminderSchema.safeParse(), but the underlying file access pattern is inconsistent.

Files:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-service.ts

Root cause: These methods iterate over multiple files in a directory and read each one. The original implementation predates StateService and was never migrated.
acceptance_criteria:
1. listReminders() uses stateService.read() with StagedReminderSchema instead of direct readFile()
2. listConsumedReminders() uses stateService.read() with StagedReminderSchema instead of direct readFile()
3. No direct fs.readFile calls remain in staging-service.ts for reading reminder JSON files
4. Existing tests pass
5. Build and typecheck pass
